content.full_text:
FAIR PoLITICAL PRACTICES CoMMISSION 
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329 
Johanna N. Canlas 
City Attorney 
City of Solana Beach 
635 South Highway IOI 
Solana Beach, CA 92075-2215 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-15-010 
Dear Ms. Canlas: 
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886 
February 4, 2015 
This letter responds to your request for advice on behalf of Councilmember Peter Zahn 
regarding the conflict of interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1 
Please note that we are only providing advice under the conflict of interest provisions of 
the Act and not under other general conflict of interest prohibitions such as common law conflict 
of interest or Section 1090. 
QUESTION 
Does Solana Beach Councilmember Zahn have a conflict of interest under the Act that 
prohibit-. him from participating in a council decision regarding a new development project? 
CONCLUSION 
No. Councilmember Zahn does not have a financial intere'it in the decision. The Act 
therefore does not prohibit hi!> participation. 
FACTS 
You are the City Attorney for the City of Solana Beach in San Diego County. You write 
on behalf of Councilmember Zahn regarding a decision coming before the council on a new 
1 Thi.! Political Reform Act is contained in Go,crnmcnt Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory 
reference arc to the Government Code. unless othcrnisc indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices 
Commission arc coniaini.!d m Sections 181 to through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All 
rcgulutor} references arc 10 Title 2. Division 6 of the Cali form a Code of Regulations. unless otherwise indicated. 


File No. A-15-010 
Page No. 2 
development project. H.G. Fenton Company is an applicant for discretionary land use permits to 
develop its property, Solana Highlands Apartments, located within the jurisdiction of the City of 
Solana Beach. The President and CEO of H.G. Fenton Company, Mike Neal, and 
Councilmember Zahn serve together on the Advisory Board of the Zahn Center for Innovation 
(Zahn Innovation Center) at San Diego State University (SDSU). Zahn Innovation Center Board 
Members are volunteers and are not compensated. 
The Zahn Innovation Center is named after Councilmember Zahn's father, Irwin Zahn, 
and was established with a gift of $725,000 from the Moxie Foundation and an additional gift of 
$150,000 from the Kerr Family. More recently H.G. Fenton Company made a donation to the 
Campanile Foundation (SDS U) for the establishment of the H.G. Fenton Idea Lab, a prototyping 
facility that is part of the Zahn Innovation Center. In addition to serving on the Zahn Innovation 
Center Board, Councilmember Zahn is the current President and a member of the Board of 
Directors of the Moxie Foundation. Councilmember Zahn is not employed by H.G. Fenton 
Company and has not received any income from H.G. Fenton Company. Councilmember Zahn 
does not hold any stock or hold any positions with H.G. Fenton Company. Moreover, the project 
area is not within 500 feet of any real property owned by Councilmember Zahn. 
ANALYSIS 
The Act's conflict-of-interest provisions ensure that public officials will "perform their 
duties in an impartial manner, free from bias caused by their own financial interests or the 
financial interests of persons who have supported them." (Section 8100l(b).) Section 87100 
prohibits any public official from making, participating in making, or otherwise using his or her 
official position to influence a governmental decision in which the official has an interest 
specified in Section 87103. They are: 
(a) Any business entity in which the public official has a direct 
or indirect investment worth $2,000 or more. 
(b) Any real property in which the public official has a direct or 
indirect interest worth $2,000 or more. 
(c) Any source of income, except gifts or loans by a 
commercial lending institution made in the regular course of business 
on terms available to the public without regard to official status, 
aggregating five hundred dollars ($500) or more in value provided or 
promised to, received by, the public official within 12 months prior to 
the time when the decision is made. 
(d) Any business entity in which the public official is a 
director, officer, partner, trustee, employee, or holds any position of 
management. 
(e) Any donor of, or any intermediary or agent for a donor of, a gift or 
gifts aggregating $460 or more in value provided to, received by, or promised to 
the public official within 12 months prior to the time when the decision is made. 


File No. A-15-010 
Page No. 3 
Under your facts, Councilmember Zahn does not have a financial interest in his volunteer 
board positions or the Zahn Innovation Center. The Act does not prohibit his participation in 
governmental decisions if he has no related financial interest. Councilmember Zahn may 
therefore participate in the decisions before the council related to H.G. Fenton Company, as 
described above. 
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660. 
HMR:jgl 
Sincerely, 
John W. Wallace 
Assistant General Counsel 
By: 
Heather M. Rowan 
Senior Counsel, Legal Division

prohibit-. him from participating in a council decision regarding a new development project?

therefore does not prohibit hi!> participation.

sections.analysis:
The Act's conflict-of-interest provisions ensure that public officials will "perform their 
duties in an impartial manner, free from bias caused by their own financial interests or the 
financial interests of persons who have supported them." (Section 8100l(b).) Section 87100 
prohibits any public official from making, participating in making, or otherwise using his or her 
official position to influence a governmental decision in which the official has an interest 
specified in Section 87103. They are: 
(a) Any business entity in which the public official has a direct 
or indirect investment worth $2,000 or more. 
(b) Any real property in which the public official has a direct or 
indirect interest worth $2,000 or more. 
(c) Any source of income, except gifts or loans by a 
commercial lending institution made in the regular course of business 
on terms available to the public without regard to official status, 
aggregating five hundred dollars ($500) or more in value provided or 
promised to, received by, the public official within 12 months prior to 
the time when the decision is made. 
(d) Any business entity in which the public official is a 
director, officer, partner, trustee, employee, or holds any position of 
management. 
(e) Any donor of, or any intermediary or agent for a donor of, a gift or 
gifts aggregating $460 or more in value provided to, received by, or promised to 
the public official within 12 months prior to the time when the decision is made.  
Under your facts, Councilmember Zahn does not have a financial interest in his volunteer 
board positions or the Zahn Innovation Center. The Act does not prohibit his participation in 
governmental decisions if he has no related financial interest. Councilmember Zahn may 
therefore participate in the decisions before the council related to H.G. Fenton Company, as 
described above.
